description: §14.8.9 declassification — the RUNTIME half of item 4 (ss60-parked, adapter-gated). reveal("col") is the sole admit path: a server fn that returns u.reveal("passwordHash") declassifies that column at this value, so the E-ADAPTER server-eval roundtrip lands the FULL row on the client (@user = {id, name, passwordHash}). This is the client-visible PRESENCE counterpart to strip-client-visible-runtime's client-visible ABSENCE — proving reveal admits the named column past the sink at runtime, not just that it suppresses the compile-time gate.
rationale: §14.8.9 (SPEC.md E-PROTECT-004 row): 'the column is not reveal-declassified ... Resolution: declassify explicitly with value.reveal("col").' The reveal stamp on the row's provenance descriptor makes _scrml_protect_redact ADMIT passwordHash, so the client observes it present. The strip machinery still engages (I-PROTECT-STRIP-001, info) — reveal admits the named column, it does not disarm the sink for the rest.
